sections.markdown:
# Structures with DEFSTRUCT

`defstruct` defines a lightweight record type. A single form generates:

* a constructor `MAKE-<name>` (keyword-based),
* a recogniser `<name>-P`,
* an accessor `<name>-<slot>` for each slot,
* a copier `COPY-<name>`.

```lisp
(defstruct point x y)

(defparameter *p* (make-point :x 3 :y 4))
(point-x *p*)         ; => 3
(setf (point-y *p*) 99)
```

Slots may have defaults: `(defstruct person (name "") (age 0))`. `defstruct` also supports `:include`, `:type`, and `:conc-name` for more advanced shapes, but the simple form is the daily bread.

sections.explain: Default printed representation uses #S read syntax.
Slots are places: (SETF (ACCESSOR OBJ) VAL) mutates.
:conc-name chooses a different prefix for accessors.

sections.prompt: Define a struct BOOK with slots TITLE and AUTHOR, then define MAKE-BOOK-SUMMARY that returns the string "TITLE by AUTHOR" for a given book.
Define a struct VEC3 with slots X, Y, Z (defaults 0). Define VEC3-SUM that returns X+Y+Z.
